methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Ark Robotics is a seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 private and 2 public peers in the same sector, with the peer-median method applied across 4 direct comparables at seed and Series A stages: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M seed).

The peer-median approach yields an implied valuation of $225M, with the full peer range spanning $60M to $500M. This method is applicable given Ark Robotics' stage alignment with at least one peer (Tenna Systems at seed) and sector homogeneity across the sample. The triangulated analysis, constrained to a single methodology, supports a valuation range of $191M (low) to $259M (high), centered on the $225M median.

Key uncertainties limiting precision include reliance on a single valuation method and limited seed-stage comparable data within the peer set—three of four comparables are at Series A, a later stage typically associated with higher valuations. Confirmation would require additional input from revenue multiples or DCF analysis, neither available in current data. The $191M–$259M range reflects peer-based positioning but should be stress-tested against Ark's specific traction, team, and capital efficiency relative to named peers.
